verdicts.claim: League "NLBA Minor Club League" is operated by Newfoundland and Labrador Basketball Association (PSO-run); scale: Fed by NLBA's 17 minor member clubs; team counts unpublished, but clubs field multiple teams per age/gender (U12 C Male provincials alone featured 'SJMB 3', 'CE23 Academy 5' and 'CE23 Academy 6', implying 3-6+ U12 boys teams at single clubs); software: Individual membership via RAMP (rampregistrations.com); program registration via each club's own stack (Citrus for Rock Sports clubs, TeamLinkt for Avalon, GOALLINE for Mount Pearl); league schedules/results published as static PDFs/images on NLBA's Duda site (Mile One Digital) — no interactive scheduling/stats platform (whitespace for SportsHub)
verdicts.evidence: NLBA's own /membership page enumerates exactly 17 named minor clubs (confirms count). NLBA's /minor-clubs matrix confirms the operator and the U12 C Male 2025 team names verbatim (via the actual PDF: SJMB 3, CE23 Academy 5, CE23 Academy 6, Central NL Youth Basketball, Avalon Basketball 1, Paradise Minor Basketball, NLBT 1). Software stack independently confirmed: Avalon Basketball uses TeamLinkt (app.teamlinkt.com), Mount Pearl Minor Basketball uses GOALLINE (site4079.goalline.ca) plus RAMP, and Rock Sports/St. John's Minor Basketball uses Citrus (register.citruscamps.com). The site is confirmed Duda-built (raw HTML has 'dmRespRow'/'dmwidget' Duda CSS classes) and footer-credits 'A Mile One Digital Inc.' (mileonedigital.com) as builder. HOWEVER: fetching the actual results pages (/u10-competitive, /u12-female, /u12-male, /u14-male, /u14-female, /u16-male, /u16-Female) shows each embeds a published Google Sheets iframe (docs.google.com/spreadsheets/.../pubhtml, one tab/gid per division) — not a static PDF or image. Only the separate /ClubLeagueSchedules page (gym-time doc) is a static PNG image. So 'results published as static PDFs/images' is incorrect for results specifically (schedules, yes; results, no — they're live-embedded Google Sheets).
verdicts.correction: Results (as opposed to schedules) are not static PDFs/images — every division's results/standings page (u10-competitive, u12-male/female, u14-male/female, u16-male/Female) embeds a published Google Sheets iframe (same spreadsheet, different gid per division). Only the master schedule (gym-time document) is a static image. The broader 'whitespace, no real interactive stats platform' conclusion still holds since a pasted Google Sheet isn't a dedicated scheduling/stats product, but the specific 'static PDFs/images' description of results is wrong. All other elements of this claim (17 minor clubs, RAMP/Citrus/TeamLinkt/GOALLINE stack, U12 C Male 2025 team names) are independently verified accurate.

verdicts.claim: League "NLBA Minor Club Provincial Basketball Championships" is operated by Newfoundland and Labrador Basketball Association; scale: 7 teams in U12 C Male 2025 alone (two pools: Central NL Youth Basketball, CE23 Academy 5, Avalon Basketball 1, Paradise Minor, NLBT 1, CE23 Academy 6, SJMB 3); with A/B/C tiers across U10-U16 M/F this implies dozens of teams province-wide; software: RAMP for membership eligibility; brackets/schedules distributed as PDFs from NLBA's Duda CDN
verdicts.evidence: Directly fetched the actual tournament PDF (irp.cdn-website.com/08525354/files/uploaded/U12_C_Male_Club.pdf — the same Duda CDN ID '08525354' that serves the rest of the NLBA site's images) for the '2025 NLBA U12 C Male Minor Club Provincial Basketball Championships,' May 2-4, 2025 at NL Sports Centre/CE23 Academy. It lists exactly the 7 teams named in the claim, split Division A (Central NL Youth Basketball, Avalon Basketball 1, Paradise Minor Basketball) and Division B (CE23 Academy 5, Next Level Basketball Training 1 [NLBT 1], CE23 Academy 6, St. John's Minor Basketball 3 [SJMB 3]). RAMP is independently confirmed as NLBA's membership platform (Canada Basketball/RAMP Interactive partnership; Mount Pearl's rampregistrations.com link).

verdicts.claim: League "NL Games basketball — regional qualifiers (Newfoundland and Labrador Winter Games)" is operated by NLBA (qualifier hosting) feeding the multi-sport NL Games; scale: ?; software: Hosting applications via form on NLBA Duda site; NLBA membership via RAMP
verdicts.evidence: Fetched NLBA's own /hostNLGamesQualifier page: it is a hosting-application form (region, gender, contact, school/city fields) for NL Games regional qualifier tournaments, run by NLBA, embedded directly on the Duda-built site. RAMP membership confirmed as above.

verdicts.claim: League "Northpoint Sports Basketball League" is operated by Northpoint Sports (private facility, 42 O'Leary Avenue, St. John's); scale: ?; software: League Standings and League Statistics pages hosted on northpointsports.com; registration via on-site web forms (no third-party registration platform identified)
verdicts.evidence: Independent listing (localgymsandfitness.com) corroborates Northpoint Sports' address as 42 O'Leary Avenue, St. John's, NL A1B 2C7. northpointsports.com/basketball-league does have 'League Standings' and 'League Statistics' section headings, matching the claim, and no external registration platform (RAMP/TeamLinkt/etc.) was found associated with it — booking is via booking.cojilio.com/booking.page for court time, consistent with an in-house/on-site model. Note: available evidence suggests Northpoint's basketball league (Men's Alumni, Women's Summer) skews adult rather than strictly youth, though the claim itself doesn't specify age group so this isn't a contradiction.

verdicts.claim: League "BNB-sanctioned club tournament circuit (winter weekends)" is operated by BNB member clubs (host); listed/sanctioned by Basketball New Brunswick; scale: 2025-26 listing includes: November Knockout (Fredericton Fusion, 28 teams, $450), Battle on the Bay (Lancaster - ~500 players per LMBA site, $500), Royal Rumble (RMBA), Winter Surge Classic + Surge jamborees (Quispamsis), February Frenzy (River Hoops, Miramichi), Thunder Tournament + Sleigh the Courts (Woodstock), Sussex Winter Classic, Holiday Rush (River Valley Rush), New Years Invitational (Grand Manan); typical 4-8 teams/event, $100-$500 entry; software: Central listing = Google Form + BNB RAMP CMS page; hosts self-manage (no common tournament platform observed - whitespace)
verdicts.correction: The 'no common tournament platform observed - whitespace' assertion is contradicted: Fredericton Fusion (host of the November Knockout listed here) actually runs its tournaments — November Knockout and the Fusion Winter Classic — on TeamLinkt (leagues.teamlinkt.com/fybafrederictonfusion/NovemberKnockout and .../FusionWinterClassic2024), a common third-party youth-sports tournament platform used elsewhere in this same NB/Atlantic dataset. That is a real, independently observable exception to 'hosts self-manage with no common platform.' Separately, the Battle on the Bay figure IS independently corroborated — lancasterhoops.com (Lancaster Minor Basketball Association's own, non-BNB site) states the tournament draws '48 teams... more than 500 competitive basketball players,' matching the claim's ~500-players figure closely. However, several other named events (Royal Rumble/RMBA, Winter Surge Classic/Quispamsis, February Frenzy, Thunder Tournament, Sleigh the Courts, Sussex Winter Classic, Holiday Rush, New Years Invitational/Grand Manan) could not be found on any independent site at all — only on BNB's own tournament-listing page — so their existence/details remain unconfirmed by outside sources.
verdicts.evidence: See correction.

verdicts.claim: League "Moncton Basketball Association (MBA) recreational + spring leagues" is operated by Moncton Basketball Association (non-profit; BNB member); scale: Spring 2026 RAMP divisions show ~45+ teams (e.g. 7 U11 boys, 6 U9 boys, 6 U14 boys rec teams plus provincial squads); year-round fall + spring sessions + summer camps; software: RAMP CMS website (monctonbasketball.ca) + RAMP Registration (MonctonBasketball.rampregistrations.com); full RAMP league engine (games/standings/leaders per division)

RAMP division/leaders pages (e.g. 'U17 Rec Boys - Moncton Basketball Association') confirm the full RAMP league engine. This is MBA's own site (not independent of MBA itself, the claim's subject), but the specific checkable figure verified exactly and nothing contradicts the rest.

verdicts.claim: League "MKMBA - Moncton Kiwanis Minor Basketball Association (LEGACY)" is operated by Moncton Kiwanis Minor Basketball Association; scale: ?; software: COMPETITIVE INTEL - legacy stack: Pointstreak Hoopstats (mkmba.hoopstats.pointstreak.com), itSportsNet league engine (itsportsnet.com leagueID=15773), SportsEngine org page; all now stale

Consistent with 'stale': MKMBA's current live presence is a separate WP-Engine-hosted site (mkmba.ca) plus active Twitter/Instagram, suggesting the old stats platforms have been superseded/abandoned rather than actively used. Nothing found contradicts 'stale.'

verdicts.claim: League "PEI Mini Rep League" is operated by Basketball PEI (PSO-run; teams entered by member minor associations); scale: Teams drawn from the 11 member minor basketball associations; per-division team counts not published. BPEI's own minor-programs/minor-associations pages and multiple individual member-club pages (Charlottetown Tigers, Cornwall Comets, Souris, East Prince Sting, Kensington, North Shore Ospreys, Stratford Foxes) corroborate the member-association structure feeding the league. verdicts.claim: League "Red Rock Classic (annual spring club tournament)" is operated by Basketball PEI; scale: ~10 divisions, roughly 4-8 teams each; draws NS/NB club programs (Fury, 506 Elite, Preston Elite, Metro Elite, Reds, UBB, Royals etc.) plus PEI's Island Aces and provincial teams; software: Schedule/results published as a shared Google Sheet linked from the BPEI events page
verdicts.evidence: Independently corroborated across several third-party sources: UPEI's own event pages (2012, 2015) confirm UPEI co-hosts the tournament; Sport PEI's news site independently lists tournament dates; a 2011 tournament schedule document (hosted on Yumpu, independent of BPEI) shows the exact format described — U14-U18 divisions with New Brunswick and Nova Scotia club teams (e.g., Bathurst U18, Codiac Storm, Saint John Selects) alongside PEI teams; a third-party Facebook video independently confirms New Brunswick club '506 Elite sent four teams' to a recent Red Rock Classic; PEI's 'Island Aces' basketball program independently confirmed to exist via its own Facebook/Instagram/X presence. The precise current-year figures ('~10 divisions, 4-8 teams each') and the 'Google Sheet' schedule-hosting detail were not independently confirmed for a recent edition (the only detailed schedule found was from 2011, showing 6 divisions of 2-5 teams), but nothing contradicts the described scale or software, and named NS/NB clubs like Fury/Preston Elite/Metro Elite/UBB/Royals are real active Maritime club programs consistent with this circuit.
